titleStmt.title: Letter from Julia Conroy to Lord Wimborne, c. May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Julia Conroy

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Julia Conroy to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, Sir Ivor Churchill Guest (1873–1939), Lord Wimborne. The lord lieutenant or viceroy was the monarch’s representative in Ireland. Conroy is appealing to the Lord lieutenant as a last attempt to obtain a pension which she claims is owed to her. Conroy claims to have received an injury in work which has left her unable to carry out manual labour. Conroy is asking for Wimborne's help in getting her some form of benefit. This letter includes an enclosure of a doctors note, it states the injury and how it was obtained.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/8064/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
I Ack simply.
II Inspectors of Lunatics
for report please J.J.T. 12/v/16 6 Cottage Garroon
Mountmellick
Queens County To His Excellancy Lord Lieutenant
of Ireland Your excellancy I Julia Conroy
humbly petition your excellancy
to give kindly consideration to my
case at your earlist convenance I
feel reluctant to truble you at the
present crisis as I am well aware of the
strenuous work you are engaged in. The facts of my case are briefly :- I
occupied the position of Female attendant
in Maryborough District Asylum for a
period of nineteen years. Some seven
years ago I met with a accident
in the execution of my duty and
although I recovered from the effects
at the time, I have since been
unable to do any manual work. as
I left the house on getting married some
time after the accident, I received no
pension nor did I receive any compensation
Benefits. I applied to The asylum committee Some
time back and The unanimusly entertained
my application but I was statute barred as
I did not apply sooner. I did not like to truble
The committee previous to this as I thought I
would get alright, but as my arm has become
useless and my husband in delicate health,
I am compelled to seek redress I
would feel very grateful to your Excellancy
if you would kindly consider my
case and try to obtain some small
pension or compensation for me.
A great many of my relatives are in
the army and I have no one to look to
only Your Excellancy. I remain
Your most humble Servant Julia Conroy
40 Moore St 6th Dec 15 This is to certify that
I have attended Mrs. Julia
Conroy for synovitis
of the elbow joint
& periostitis off & on
for the past 6 six years,
which originated in
a fall received in the
Maryboro Asylum.
Signed M.L. O'Brien M.D.
